name: Gary's Chocolate Cream Pie
recipeInstructions:
Make a double batch of Gary's Chocolate Pudding, increasing the cornstarch to about 4 1/2 tablespoons per batch (8-10 total). Chill thoroughly. This pudding will be extremely thick.
Make or purchase your favorite pie shell. I personally like the good old fashioned, plain pie shell. My family seems to prefer a cookie type of shell. If you make your own, pre-bake it as this pie requires NO BAKING.
Whip cream with confectioner's sugar and vanilla extract until thick and fluffy. Take half of the whipped cream and gently fold into the chocolate pudding. Because the pudding is so thick, use an electric mixer for best results. Mix until all of the whipped cream is thoroughly incorporated.
Fill pie shell with the pudding-whipped cream mixture. Place remaining whipped cream into a piping bag and pipe onto top of pie. Shave bittersweet chocolate on top as garnish.
Cover pie and place in refrigerator for 2-3 hours to allow the cornstarch from the confectioner's sugar to set.
